summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that authenticated CP users can inject Twig template code through the elementIds field in condition rules. The sandbox is disabled by default (enableTwigSandbox=false), allowing full application access through craft.app including database operations via craft.app.db.createCommand().
expected_security_impact: Sending a crafted POST request to a CP element index endpoint with Twig template code in the condition rule elementIds field causes server-side Twig template evaluation with full application access. A Twig error payload triggers a 500 response while a control string returns 200, proving SSTI. A second payload executes SQL through craft.app.db to demonstrate RCE.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh builds a Docker container with Craft CMS 5.9.8, installs the application with MySQL, authenticates as a CP user, and sends crafted POST requests. The Twig error payload ({{ 1/0 }}) returns HTTP 500 while the control payload returns HTTP 200, confirming SSTI. An RCE payload creates a database table via craft.app.db.createCommand() through the SSTI, which is verified by querying the database directly.
verification_method: The verification compares HTTP response status codes: a Twig error payload causes HTTP 500 (Twig RuntimeError from division by zero) while a non-Twig control string returns HTTP 200. Additionally, a database table created through craft.app.db in the SSTI payload is verified by querying MySQL directly after the exploit request.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP 500 response when sending Twig error payload {{ 1/0 }} proves the input is rendered as a Twig template (SSTI).
HTTP 200 response when sending a plain text control string proves the non-error path works normally.
Database query confirms a table (evohunt_proof) was created through the SSTI payload via craft.app.db.createCommand(), proving RCE.
The response code differential (500 vs 200) confirms server-side Twig template injection through the elementIds condition rule field.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable version (5.9.8 tag), and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
